LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JUAN RODRIGUEZ'S MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE IS GRANTED. COUNSEL MUST PROVIDE A PROPOSED ORDER STATING THAT THE CLASS CLAIMS ARE DISMISSED WITHOUT PREJUDICE; THE MOTION IS GRANTED BASED ON THE SWORN REPRESENTATION THAT NO CONSIDERATION, DIRECT OR INDIRECT, WAS PROVIDED TO PLAINTIFF OR COUNSEL; AND THAT NO NOTICE TO THE PUTATIVE CLASS IS REQUIRED BECAUSE THE PUTATIVE CLASS MEMBERS WILL NOT BE PREJUDICED BY THE LACK OF NOTICE. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED. (302/CK)

Motion To Dismiss Action Without Prejudice
Off Calendar Mar-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JUAN RODRIGUEZ, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL Motion To Dismiss Action Without Prejudice

No appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JUAN RODRIGUEZ'S Motion To Dismiss Action Without Prejudice is Granted.

Counsel must provide a proposed order stating that the class claims are dismissed without prejudice; the motion is granted based on the sworn representation that no consideration, direct or indirect, was provided to plaintiff or counsel; and that no notice to the putative class is required because the putative class members will not be prejudiced by the lack of notice.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/CK)
